title: Compound effects of extreme heat and wildfire-PM2.5 air pollution on child hospitalizations in multiple countries
keywords: relative excess risk due to interaction
methods: relative excess risk due to interaction (RERI)

data_scope.spatial_coverage: 954 study communities across 5 countries (Australia, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Thailand)
data_scope.population: children and adolescents aged 19 years or younger
data_scope.exposure_definitions: EHE = 2+ consecutive days mean temp > 90th pct; HFPP = PM2.5 > 15 ug/m3 with >=50% from wildfires; HNPP = non-fire high PM2.5
data_scope.hospitalization_access: restricted (data-custodian agreements; on request from corresponding author)

core_conclusion: Across 954 communities in five countries (2000-2019), days when an extreme heat event and a high-level wildfire-PM2.5 pollution day co-occurred carried a 3.8% higher risk of all-cause child hospitalization (RR=1.038, 1.029-1.048), greater than either hazard alone. The interaction was synergistic (positive RERI) for respiratory, renal and diabetes admissions, and consistently stronger than the heat x non-fire-PM2.5 combination.
why_it_matters: Among the first multi-country quantifications of the compound effect of heat plus wildfire smoke specifically on children; distinguishes wildfire PM2.5 as more harmful than ordinary PM2.5; extends recognised harm beyond respiratory disease to renal and diabetes admissions; identifies vulnerable subgroups for targeted paediatric adaptation.
why_nature: Harmonised five-country, 954-community, three-continent design; links a concrete climate driver to disease-specific paediatric outcomes and adaptation; rigorous causal-effect estimation (quasi-Poisson, two-stage meta-analysis, RERI with Monte-Carlo); strong equity and vulnerability lens.
added_value_to_my_research: Compound-risk x accessibility surface -> hazard-responsive 2SFCA/R2SFCA paediatric service-area model
Explainable ML (SHAP/GAM) for nonlinear heat x wildfire synergy -> informed priors for MGWR/GWR
Physics-informed graph operator over airshed-connected communities -> forecastable compound-exposure early warning
Equity-aware allocator for clean-air shelters/cooling refuges -> efficiency-vs-equity optimization extension
Mobility + sensor fusion for realised child exposure -> multi-source data-fusion FAIR pipeline
